content_of_post_to_answer: If we can never experience reality as it is, only as our mind interprets it, then is anyone truly living in the same world?
answer: what we call the same world is actually a negotiated overlap of private prediction models. each mind filters reality through memory, attention, and expectation, so experience diverges at the edges but converges where feedback forces alignment. disagreement isn't proof of different worlds, but of different compression of the same signals; shared reality is a constantly corrected intersection, not a single view.
